Today we’re excited to release RapidWeaver Elements 3.0 as a public beta. It’s a free update for everyone with an active license.
Elements 3.0 moves to the Tailwind 4 CSS framework and adds 3 new components, along with a host of new features and improvements. Later in the beta cycle, we’ll also be releasing Elements AI and the Elements MCP server.
Watch the video below for a preview of just some of what’s new in Elements 3, it’s our biggest release yet. We can’t wait to hear what you think!
What’s New in Elements 3.0 Beta?

• Upgraded to Tailwind 4.3.2

• Updated all themes to use Tailwind 4

• Updated border radius and shadow tokens to match Tailwind 4

• Updated opacity utilities to use Tailwind 4 opacity modifiers

• Added Mauve, Olive, Mist, and Taupe colours from Tailwind 4 to all themes

• Global controls now use Tailwind 4 syntax and generate cleaner spacing classes

• New Mask component

• New 3D Transforms component

• New Shapes component that lets text flow around shapes!

• Added caption and author support to the Gallery Lightbox

• Added CSV support (local or remote) to the Table component

• Added Google Sheets support to the Table component

• Added Drop Zone support to the Table component

• Added box-shadow colour and opacity controls

• Added support for radial and conical gradients

• Added support for custom HTML attributes on the Container component

• Added a new requiresPhp key to the Elements API

• Components that require PHP now prompt to switch the page extension from html to php

• Improved the CMS Online Editor UI on mobile devices

• Online CMS Metadata sidebar is now resizable, with an increased maximum width

• Fixed an issue when creating new posts in the online CMS

• Upgraded the editor styling engine to reduce CSS conflicts and improve isolation

• Added a Markdown Preview option to the editor

• Fixed white edge lines appearing on dark JPEGs in the edit-mode preview

• Fixed missing global override unlink for Switch and Checkbox inspector controls

• Numerous other bug fixes and improvements
Beta Availability
While we believe this first beta is very stable, we don’t recommend using it on live, mission-critical websites. The Tailwind 4 framework touches most parts of the app, including how webpages render, so please check your sites for layout issues or changes before re-publishing.
To install the beta, launch your current build of Elements and go to Settings/Preferences. Under the Updates section, enable beta updates. Once beta updates are enabled, go to the Elements menu and select “Check for Updates…”.
The Future of Web Design
Thanks for being part of Elements and for helping us shape the future of web design on the Mac. Your feedback during the beta will play a big role in where Elements goes from here.
We’re excited to keep building, refining, and shipping, and we really can’t wait to show you what’s coming next.
Thanks,
Team Elements
—
